name: pigeon-transitions

metadata.name: pigeon-transitions
metadata.summary: A state machine framework for Pigeon.

documentation:
[![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pigeon-transitions)](https://pypi.org/project/pigeon-transitions/)

# Pigeon Transitions

Pigeon transitions is a framework for creating event driven state machines using the Python [Transitions](https://github.com/pytransitions/transitions) library. This framework has the Pigeon message passing client built in.

## Documentation

An example is provided in the [example folder](https://github.com/AllenInstitute/pigeon-transitions/tree/master/example).

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine.__init__
documentation:
The standard transitions Machine constructor with the folowing changes:

* The model is disabled.
* An on_enter callback is created on the machine and transformed using _get_callables and _get_callable.
* The before_state_change, after_state_change, prepare_event, finalize_event, on_exception, and on_final
    callbacks are transformed using _get_callables and _get_callable.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine.init_child
documentation: This is a helper routine for propogating configuration to child machines.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine._create_state
documentation: Transform callbacks using _get_callables

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine.add_transition
documentation: Transform callbacks using _get_callables

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine._get_callable
documentation:
Get a class member function of the same name as the input if available.
If the class member is not a function, create a lambda function which
returns the current value of the class member variable. If the input is
a variable, return a lambda function which returns the current value of
the variable.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine._get_callables
documentation:
Returns a transformed list of callbacks with string entries substituted
for class member functions when available.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine._add_machine_states
documentation:
This method is overridden to build the parent, child relationships
between each machine in the hierarchy.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine._add_dict_state
documentation:
This method is overridden to add the child machine's on_enter
callback(s) to the parent state's on_enter callback.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine._remap_state
documentation:
This function overrides the normal _remap_state method to add the following:
* Remove the remaped state so it does not appear in the diagram.
* Add any on_enter callbacks of the remapped state to the after callbacks of
the transition.
* Allow passing a dict as the value in the remap dict which specifies the
destination, and adds specified conditions, and callbacks to the transition.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine.message_callback
documentation:
This message callback can be overridden with the desired functionality
for the machine. All machines in the hierarchy of active states will have
this method called, starting at the leaf.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine.root
documentation: Traverse the tree of hierarchical machines to the root and return it.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine.client
documentation:
Returns the Pigeon client, or None, if the machine is not part of the
current state.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine.get_state_path
documentation:
Returns the hierarchical state that leads to this machine.

If join is False, returns a list of hierarchical states which lead to
    this machine.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine.get_machine_state
documentation:
Returns the current state of this machine, or None, if the current
state is not a state in this machine, or a substate.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine._current_machine
documentation: Returns True if the current state is a state of this machine, or a substate.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine.current_machine
documentation: Returns True if the current state is a state of this machine strictly.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.BaseMachine.__getattr__
documentation:
If a class attribute is not available in this class, try to get it
from the root class.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine.__init__
documentation:
This constructor builds on the BaseMachine constructor adding the following:

* The state machine model is the class instance.
* Auto transitions are disabled.
* State attributes (callbacks etc.), and transition conditions and callbacks are
    configured to be displayed on the graph.
* Active and previous states are styled the same as the inactive states on the graph.
* A logger is created if not provided.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine._init_graphviz_engine
documentation:
This method takes the existing graph class selected based on what
graph engine and creates and returns a subclass.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine.add_client
documentation:
This method adds a Pigeon client to the class, and subscribes to all
known messages.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine.save_graph
documentation:
This method saves a graph of the hierarchical state machine to the
provided path.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine._get_machine
documentation: This method returns the machine instance which a given state is part of.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine._get_current_machine
documentation: This method returns the machine instance which the current state is part of.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine._get_current_machines
documentation:
This generator first yields the full hierarchical state of the current
machine then continues yielding states descending to the root machine.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine._message_callback
documentation:
This method is the main callback for Pigeon messages. It stores the
most recent message on each topic, and it takes the message and calls
the message_callback function in each machine, starting at the leaf,
and traversing to the root.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine._collect
documentation: This method stores the most recent message recieved on each topic.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine.get_collected
documentation: This function returns the most recent message recieved on a given topic.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine._get_initial_states
documentation: This method returns the set of initial states of the hierarchical state machine.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine._start
documentation:
This method can be called at the beginning of execution of the state
machine. It runs the on_enter callback of each of the machines that are
part of the initial state.

methods.name: pigeon_transitions.base.RootMachine._run
documentation: This method runs the _start routine, then enters an infinte loop.
